name: Space Fleets — Desert Power
description: 10 vehicles — Exotic spacecraft from settings where technology bends towards the strange: ornithopters that ride desert thermals, vast guild ships that fold space, psionic war vessels, and swarm organisms that consume worlds.

vehicles.name: Ornithopter (Military)
vehicles.desc: Wing-beating atmospheric craft that combines ancient aerodynamic principles with advanced materials science. She can hover, manoeuvre through canyon systems, and land on surfaces that would destroy conventional aircraft. In the desert kingdoms where shields make projectile weapons suicidal, she carries laser weapons and troops for rapid insertion. Her articulated wings make her look alive, and her pilots fly her like a living thing.
vehicles.notes: Atmospheric only. Wing-beating flight. Carries 6-8 troops. Desert environment.

vehicles.name: Spice Harvester
vehicles.desc: Enormous industrial crawler that processes desert sand to extract the most valuable substance in the universe. She's slow, unarmed, and completely dependent on aerial spotters to warn her when the great worms approach. A harvester lost to a worm costs more than a warship, and the political consequences of losing one's spice production can topple governments. Her crew works in shifts, always watching the sand.
vehicles.notes: Industrial. Unarmed. Atmospheric. Must be extracted by carryall before worm attacks.

vehicles.name: Carryall
vehicles.desc: Heavy-lift atmospheric transport designed to grab harvesters and evacuate them before sandworm attacks. She's the largest aircraft in regular service, powerful enough to lift thousands of tonnes, and her pilots have nerves of steel and reflexes to match. A carryall pilot who drops a harvester doesn't survive the political fallout even if they survive the crash.
vehicles.notes: Heavy-lift transport. Atmospheric. Can carry a harvester. Unarmed.

vehicles.name: Guild Heighliner
vehicles.desc: Vessel of incomprehensible scale, capable of folding space to move entire fleets between star systems instantaneously. She is operated by the Navigator's Guild — mutated humans who perceive the fabric of reality through prescient awareness. No weapons, no armour, and no need for either. Attacking a heighliner would strand the attacker forever. The Guild's monopoly on interstellar travel is the foundation of galactic civilisation, and they know it.
vehicles.notes: FTL transport. Carries entire fleets internally. Guild monopoly. Unarmed. Untouchable.

vehicles.name: Frigate (Great Houses)
vehicles.desc: Warship of the noble houses, built for the peculiar combat doctrine of a universe where personal shields stop fast-moving projectiles. Her weapons are a mix of lasers — which interact explosively with shields — and slow-pellet stunners designed to overwhelm shield generators through sustained fire. Boarding actions are common, and her marine contingent carries bladed weapons for the close-quarters fighting that shield technology makes inevitable.
vehicles.notes: Shield doctrine. Mixed laser/kinetic weapons. Strong boarding capability.

vehicles.name: Terran Battlecruiser
vehicles.desc: Heavily armoured human warship built by a military-industrial complex that values brute force over elegance. She carries banks of conventional weapons backed by a devastating primary weapon — a focused nuclear device delivered via electromagnetic catapult. Her crew are conscripts, her officers are professionals, and her captain has standing orders to deploy the primary weapon only with admiralty authorisation. He keeps the launch key on a chain around his neck.
vehicles.notes: Starcraft archetype. Yamato cannon equivalent. Heavy conventional armament.

vehicles.name: Psionic Carrier
vehicles.desc: Elegant vessel of an advanced civilisation that channels psychic energy through crystalline hull matrices. She carries no conventional fighters — instead, her crew projects remotely-controlled energy constructs that fight with the skill of their psychic operators. Her weapons fire concentrated thought-energy that ignores physical armour but can be deflected by sufficient willpower. She is beautiful, terrifying, and utterly alien.
vehicles.notes: Protoss archetype. Psionic weapons. Interceptor drones. Energy shields.

vehicles.name: Swarm Leviathan
vehicles.desc: Living capital organism of a species that consumes biomass to fuel its expansion across the galaxy. She is not a ship in any meaningful sense — she is a creature, vast and purposeful, and the smaller organisms that swarm around her are extensions of her will. Her weapons are biological: acid projectiles, bio-plasma, and boarding organisms that infest enemy vessels and consume them from the inside out.
vehicles.notes: Zerg archetype. Living ship. Spawns smaller organisms. Consumes defeated enemies.

vehicles.name: Wraith (Stealth Fighter)
vehicles.desc: Single-seat fighter equipped with a cloaking device that renders her invisible to sensors and the naked eye. She decloaks, fires, and vanishes before the target's point defence can respond. Her pilots are recruited from the ranks of the psychically gifted, because the cloaking system requires a human mind to stabilise. Extended use causes headaches, nosebleeds, and eventually madness.
vehicles.notes: Terran archetype. Personal cloak. Burst damage. Pilot degradation over time.

vehicles.name: Void Ray
vehicles.desc: Psionic energy weapon platform that channels focused psychic energy through a prismatic crystal array. Her beam starts weak but intensifies over sustained contact, eventually cutting through any known material. She must remain stationary to maintain beam coherence, making her vulnerable to attack — but if left unchecked, she can dismantle a capital ship one section at a time with terrifying precision.
vehicles.notes: Protoss archetype. Escalating beam damage. Stationary while firing. Glass cannon.
